questions.question: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort?
questions.explanation: Selection Sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that has a time complexity of O(n^2) in the worst case scenario.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Bubble Sort?
options: It is a stable sorting algorithm
It is an in-place sorting algorithm
It is a comparison-based sorting algorithm
It is a non-comparative sorting algorithm
questions.explanation: Bubble Sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that is not stable and not in-place.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort?
questions.explanation: Bubble Sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that has a time complexity of O(n^2) in the worst case scenario.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Selection Sort?
options: It is a stable sorting algorithm
It is an in-place sorting algorithm
It is a comparison-based sorting algorithm
It is a non-comparative sorting algorithm
questions.explanation: Selection Sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that is in-place but not stable.

questions.question: What is the space complexity of both Selection Sort and Bubble Sort?
questions.explanation: Both Selection Sort and Bubble Sort have a space complexity of O(1) as they only use a constant amount of extra space.
